name: ETLAS-2 Mental Well-Being Extract
population_or_context: Patients with cerebral small vessel disease and prior stroke or transient ischemic attack; mean age about 68 years.
methods: Randomized placebo-controlled double-blind phase II trial of tadalafil 20 mg daily for 3 months versus placebo; secondary outcomes included cognition, mental well-being, blood pressure, and adverse events.
population_profile: Older CSVD patients with previous stroke or transient ischemic attack; not healthy men age 30 to 50.
study_design: Randomized placebo-controlled double-blind parallel phase II clinical trial.
safety_findings: Adverse events occurred in 76% with tadalafil versus 36% with placebo, and adherence was lower with tadalafil.
directness_to_target_population: Indirect because participants were older CSVD patients and tadalafil dose was 20 mg daily.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Mental well-being was a secondary outcome in a feasibility-primary phase II disease-population trial.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; population, dose, disease context, and secondary-outcome status limit transfer to healthy mood or well-being claims.
